description:
## What to build

Add the backend half of the first issue-list slice. Beadsmith should expose a Tauri command that reads real Beadwork issues for the current workspace by running `bw list --all --json` in the directory Beadsmith was launched from.

The command should return a narrowed `IssueSummary` list for the frontend, not the full Beadwork issue JSON. Normalize `null` from `bw` to an empty list. Missing `bw`, non-Beadwork directories, subprocess failures, and JSON parse failures should return a typed, user-displayable error rather than silently becoming an empty list.

No React layout, sidebar, search, selection, issue detail, workspace switching, or Lucide work is in scope for this backend task.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A Tauri command exists for listing all issues in the launch current working directory.
- [ ] The command invokes `bw list --all --json`; it does not use default actionable-only `bw list` behavior.
- [ ] `null` or `[]` output from `bw` becomes an empty issue summary list.
- [ ] React-facing data uses a narrowed `IssueSummary` contract with fields needed for list rendering and near-future badges.
- [ ] Missing `bw`, non-Beadwork directories, subprocess failures, and JSON parse failures are distinguishable from a true empty issue list.
- [ ] Relevant Rust checks/tests pass.

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately.

## Triage

ready-for-agent
title: Expose all issues through a Tauri IssueSummary command
